Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. 9	class PinholeCamera:
10	    """Class that represents a Pinhole Camera model.
11	
12	    Args:
13	        intrinsics: tensor with shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`
14	          containing the full 4x4 camera calibration matrix.
15	        extrinsics: tensor with shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`
16	          containing the full 4x4 rotation-translation matrix.
17	        height: tensor with shape :math:`(B)` containing the image height.
18	        width: tensor with shape :math:`(B)` containing the image width.
19	
20	    .. note::
21	        We assume that the class attributes are in batch form in order to take
22	        advantage of PyTorch parallelism to boost computing performance.
23	    """
24	
25	    def __init__(self, intrinsics: Tensor, extrinsics: Tensor, height: Tensor, width: Tensor) -> None:
26	        self._check_valid([intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width])
27	        self._check_valid_params(intrinsics, 'intrinsics')
28	        self._check_valid_params(extrinsics, 'extrinsics')
29	        self._check_valid_shape(height, 'height')
30	        self._check_valid_shape(width, 'width')
31	        self._check_consistent_device([intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width])
32	        self.height: Tensor = height
33	        self.width: Tensor = width
34	        self._intrinsics: Tensor = intrinsics
35	        self._extrinsics: Tensor = extrinsics
36	
37	    @staticmethod
38	    def _check_valid(data_iter: Iterable[Tensor]) -> bool:
39	        if not all((data.shape[0] for data in data_iter)):
40	            raise ValueError('Arguments shapes must match')
41	        return True
42	
43	    @staticmethod
44	    def _check_valid_params(data: Tensor, data_name: str) -> bool:
45	        if len(data.shape) not in (3, 4) and data.shape[-2:] != (4, 4):
46	            raise ValueError(f'Argument {data_name} shape must be in the following shape Bx4x4 or BxNx4x4. Got {data.shape}')
47	        return True
48	
49	    @staticmethod
50	    def _check_valid_shape(data: Tensor, data_name: str) -> bool:
51	        if not len(data.shape) == 1:
52	            raise ValueError(f'Argument {data_name} shape must be in the following shape B. Got {data.shape}')
53	        return True
54	
55	    @staticmethod
56	    def _check_consistent_device(data_iter: List[Tensor]) -> None:
57	        first = data_iter[0]
58	        for data in data_iter:
59	            KORNIA_CHECK_SAME_DEVICE(data, first)
60	
61	    def device(self) -> torch.device:
62	        """Returns the device for camera buffers.
63	
64	        Returns:
65	            Device type
66	        """
67	        return self._intrinsics.device
68	
69	    @property

71	        """The full 4x4 intrinsics matrix.
72	
73	        Returns:
74	            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`.
75	        """
76	        if not self._check_valid_params(self._intrinsics, 'intrinsics'):
77	            raise AssertionError

82	        """The full 4x4 extrinsics matrix.

93	        """Return the batch size of the storage.

95	        Returns:
96	            scalar with the batch size.
97	        """
98	        return self.intrinsics.shape[0]
99	
100	    @property
101	    def fx(self) -> Tensor:
102	        """Return the focal length in the x-direction.
103	
104	        Returns:
105	            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
106	        """

115	        """

118	    @property

121	
122	        Returns:

124	        """

129	        """Return the y-coordinate of the principal point.

168	        """Returns the z-coordinate of the translation vector.

192	        """Return the 3x3 camera matrix containing the intrinsics.
193	
194	        Returns:
195	            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 3)`.
196	        """

201	        """Return the 3x3 rotation matrix from the extrinsics.
202	
203	        Returns:
204	            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 3)`.
205	        """

207	
208	    @property
209	    def translation_vector(self) -> Tensor:
210	        """Return the translation vector from the extrinsics.
211	
212	        Returns:
213	            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 1)`.
214	        """
215	        return self.extrinsics[..., :3, -1:]

217	    def clone(self) -> 'PinholeCamera':
218	        """Return a deep copy of the current object instance."""
219	        height: Tensor = self.height.clone()
220	        width: Tensor = self.width.clone()
221	        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics.clone()
222	        extrinsics: Tensor = self.extrinsics.clone()
223	        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width)

225	    def intrinsics_inverse(self) -> Tensor:
226	        """Return the inverse of the 4x4 instrisics matrix.
227	
228	        Returns:
229	            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`.
230	        """
231	        return self.intrinsics.inverse()

233	    def scale(self, scale_factor: Tensor) -> 'PinholeCamera':
234	        """Scale the pinhole model.
235	
236	        Args:
237	            scale_factor: a tensor with the scale factor. It has
238	              to be broadcastable with class members. The expected shape is
239	              :math:`(B)` or :math:`(1)`.
240	
241	        Returns:
242	            the camera model with scaled parameters.
243	        """
244	        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics.clone()
245	        intrinsics[..., 0, 0] *= scale_factor
246	        intrinsics[..., 1, 1] *= scale_factor
247	        intrinsics[..., 0, 2] *= scale_factor
248	        intrinsics[..., 1, 2] *= scale_factor
249	        height: Tensor = scale_factor * self.height.clone()
250	        width: Tensor = scale_factor * self.width.clone()
251	        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, self.extrinsics, height, width)
252	
253	    def scale_(self, scale_factor: Union[float, Tensor]) -> 'PinholeCamera':
254	        """Scale the pinhole model in-place.
255	
256	        Args:
257	            scale_factor: a tensor with the scale factor. It has
258	              to be broadcastable with class members. The expected shape is
259	              :math:`(B)` or :math:`(1)`.
260	
261	        Returns:
262	            the camera model with scaled parameters.
263	        """
264	        self.intrinsics[..., 0, 0] *= scale_factor
265	        self.intrinsics[..., 1, 1] *= scale_factor
266	        self.intrinsics[..., 0, 2] *= scale_factor
267	        self.intrinsics[..., 1, 2] *= scale_factor
268	        self.height *= scale_factor
269	        self.width *= scale_factor

277	    def __init__(self, intrinsics: Tensor, extrinsics: Tensor, height: Tensor, width: Tensor) -> None:
278	        self.height: Tensor = height
279	        self.width: Tensor = width

281	        self._extrinsics: Tensor = extrinsics
282	```"'''
283	        raise NotImplementedError('DL-Bench: implement this function')

285	    def unproject(self, point_2d: Tensor, depth: Tensor) -> Tensor:
286	        """Unproject a 2d point in 3d.
287	
288	        Transform coordinates in the pixel frame to the world frame.
289	
290	        Args:
291	            point2d: tensor containing the 2d to be projected to
292	                world coordinates. The shape of the tensor can be :math:`(*, 2)`.
293	            depth: tensor containing the depth value of each 2d
294	                points. The tensor shape must be equal to point2d :math:`(*, 1)`.
295	            normalize: whether to normalize the pointcloud. This
296	                must be set to `True` when the depth is represented as the Euclidean
297	                ray length from the camera position.
298	
299	        Returns:
300	            tensor of (x, y, z) world coordinates with shape :math:`(*, 3)`.
301	
302	        Example:
303	            >>> _ = torch.manual_seed(0)
304	            >>> x = torch.rand(1, 2)
305	            >>> depth = torch.ones(1, 1)
306	            >>> K = torch.eye(4)[None]
307	            >>> E = torch.eye(4)[None]
308	            >>> h = torch.ones(1)
309	            >>> w = torch.ones(1)
310	            >>> pinhole = kornia.geometry.camera.PinholeCamera(K, E, h, w)
311	            >>> pinhole.unproject(x, depth)
312	            tensor([[0.4963, 0.7682, 1.0000]])
313	        """
314	        P = self.intrinsics @ self.extrinsics
315	        P_inv = _torch_inverse_cast(P)
316	        return transform_points(P_inv, convert_points_to_homogeneous(point_2d) * depth)

318	    @classmethod
319	    def from_parameters(self, fx: Tensor, fy: Tensor, cx: Tensor, cy: Tensor, height: int, width: int, tx: Tensor, ty: Tensor, tz: Tensor, batch_size: int, device: Device, dtype: torch.dtype) -> 'PinholeCamera':
320	        intrinsics = zeros(batch_size, 4, 4, device=device, dtype=dtype)
321	        intrinsics[..., 0, 0] += fx
322	        intrinsics[..., 1, 1] += fy
323	        intrinsics[..., 0, 2] += cx
324	        intrinsics[..., 1, 2] += cy
325	        intrinsics[..., 2, 2] += 1.0
326	        intrinsics[..., 3, 3] += 1.0
327	        extrinsics = eye(4, device=device, dtype=dtype).repeat(batch_size, 1, 1)
328	        extrinsics[..., 0, -1] += tx
329	        extrinsics[..., 1, -1] += ty
330	        extrinsics[..., 2, -1] += tz
331	        height_tmp = zeros(batch_size, device=device, dtype=dtype)
332	        height_tmp[..., 0] += height
333	        width_tmp = zeros(batch_size, device=device, dtype=dtype)
334	        width_tmp[..., 0] += width
335	        return self(intrinsics, extrinsics, height_tmp, width_tmp)

337	class PinholeCamerasList(PinholeCamera):
338	    """Class that represents a list of pinhole cameras.
339	
340	    The class inherits from :class:`~kornia.PinholeCamera` meaning that
341	    it will keep the same class properties but with an extra dimension.
342	
343	    .. note::
344	        The underlying data tensor will be stacked in the first dimension.
345	        That's it, given a list of two camera instances, the intrinsics tensor
346	        will have a shape :math:`(B, N, 4, 4)` where :math:`B` is the batch
347	        size and :math:`N` is the numbers of cameras (in this case two).
348	
349	    Args:
350	        pinholes_list: a python tuple or list containing a set of `PinholeCamera` instances.
351	    """
352	
353	    def __init__(self, pinholes_list: Iterable[PinholeCamera]) -> None:
354	        self._initialize_parameters(pinholes_list)
355	
356	    def _initialize_parameters(self, pinholes: Iterable[PinholeCamera]) -> 'PinholeCamerasList':
357	        """Initialise the class attributes given a cameras list."""
358	        if not isinstance(pinholes, (list, tuple)):
359	            raise TypeError(f'pinhole must of type list or tuple. Got {type(pinholes)}')
360	        (height, width) = ([], [])
361	        (intrinsics, extrinsics) = ([], [])
362	        for pinhole in pinholes:
363	            if not isinstance(pinhole, PinholeCamera):
364	                raise TypeError(f'Argument pinhole must be from type PinholeCamera. Got {type(pinhole)}')
365	            height.append(pinhole.height)
366	            width.append(pinhole.width)
367	            intrinsics.append(pinhole.intrinsics)
368	            extrinsics.append(pinhole.extrinsics)
369	        self.height: Tensor = stack(height, dim=1)
370	        self.width: Tensor = stack(width, dim=1)
371	        self._intrinsics: Tensor = stack(intrinsics, dim=1)
372	        self._extrinsics: Tensor = stack(extrinsics, dim=1)
373	        return self
374	
375	    @property
376	    def num_cameras(self) -> int:
377	        """Return the number of pinholes cameras per batch."""
378	        num_cameras: int = -1
379	        if self.intrinsics is not None:
380	            num_cameras = int(self.intrinsics.shape[1])
381	        return num_cameras
382	
383	    def get_pinhole(self, idx: int) -> PinholeCamera:
384	        """Return a PinholeCamera object with parameters such as Bx4x4."""
385	        height: Tensor = self.height[..., idx]
386	        width: Tensor = self.width[..., idx]
387	        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics[:, idx]
388	        extrinsics: Tensor = self.extrinsics[:, idx]
389	        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width)
390	
391	def pinhole_matrix(pinholes: Tensor, eps: float=1e-06) -> Tensor:
392	    """Function that returns the pinhole matrix from a pinhole model.
393	
394	    .. note::
395	        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
396	        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.camera_matrix`.
397	
398	    Args:
399	        pinholes: tensor of pinhole models.
400	
401	    Returns:
402	        tensor of pinhole matrices.
403	
404	    Shape:
405	        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)`
406	        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`
407	
408	    Example:
409	        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
410	        >>> pinhole = torch.rand(1, 12)    # Nx12
411	        >>> pinhole_matrix(pinhole)  # Nx4x4
412	        tensor([[[4.9626e-01, 1.0000e-06, 8.8477e-02, 1.0000e-06],
413	                 [1.0000e-06, 7.6822e-01, 1.3203e-01, 1.0000e-06],
414	                 [1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e+00, 1.0000e-06],
415	                 [1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e+00]]])
416	    """
417	    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
418	        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
419	    (fx, fy, cx, cy) = torch.chunk(pinholes[..., :4], 4, dim=1)
420	    k = eye(4, device=pinholes.device, dtype=pinholes.dtype) + eps
421	    k = k.view(1, 4, 4).repeat(pinholes.shape[0], 1, 1)
422	    k[..., 0, 0:1] = fx
423	    k[..., 0, 2:3] = cx
424	    k[..., 1, 1:2] = fy
425	    k[..., 1, 2:3] = cy
426	    return k
427	
428	def inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole: Tensor, eps: float=1e-06) -> Tensor:
429	    """Return the inverted pinhole matrix from a pinhole model.
430	
431	    .. note::
432	        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
433	        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.intrinsics_inverse()`.
434	
435	    Args:
436	        pinholes: tensor with pinhole models.
437	
438	    Returns:
439	        tensor of inverted pinhole matrices.
440	
441	    Shape:
442	        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)`
443	        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`
444	
445	    Example:
446	        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
447	        >>> pinhole = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
448	        >>> inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole)  # Nx4x4
449	        tensor([[[ 2.0151,  0.0000, -0.1783,  0.0000],
450	                 [ 0.0000,  1.3017, -0.1719,  0.0000],
451	                 [ 0.0000,  0.0000,  1.0000,  0.0000],
452	                 [ 0.0000,  0.0000,  0.0000,  1.0000]]])
453	    """
454	    if not (len(pinhole.shape) == 2 and pinhole.shape[1] == 12):
455	        raise AssertionError(pinhole.shape)
456	    (fx, fy, cx, cy) = torch.chunk(pinhole[..., :4], 4, dim=1)
457	    k = eye(4, device=pinhole.device, dtype=pinhole.dtype)
458	    k = k.view(1, 4, 4).repeat(pinhole.shape[0], 1, 1)
459	    k[..., 0, 0:1] = 1.0 / (fx + eps)
460	    k[..., 1, 1:2] = 1.0 / (fy + eps)
461	    k[..., 0, 2:3] = -1.0 * cx / (fx + eps)
462	    k[..., 1, 2:3] = -1.0 * cy / (fy + eps)
463	    return k
464	
465	def scale_pinhole(pinholes: Tensor, scale: Tensor) -> Tensor:
466	    """Scale the pinhole matrix for each pinhole model.
467	
468	    .. note::
469	        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
470	        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.scale()`.
471	
472	    Args:
473	        pinholes: tensor with the pinhole model.
474	        scale: tensor of scales.
475	
476	    Returns:
477	        tensor of scaled pinholes.
478	
479	    Shape:
480	        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)` and :math:`(N, 1)`
481	        - Output: :math:`(N, 12)`
482	
483	    Example:
484	        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
485	        >>> pinhole_i = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
486	        >>> scales = 2.0 * torch.ones(1)   # N
487	        >>> scale_pinhole(pinhole_i, scales)  # Nx12
488	        tensor([[0.9925, 1.5364, 0.1770, 0.2641, 0.6148, 1.2682, 0.4901, 0.8964, 0.4556,
489	                 0.6323, 0.3489, 0.4017]])
490	    """
491	    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
492	        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
493	    if len(scale.shape) != 1:
494	        raise AssertionError(scale.shape)
495	    pinholes_scaled = pinholes.clone()
496	    pinholes_scaled[..., :6] = pinholes[..., :6] * scale.unsqueeze(-1)
497	    return pinholes_scaled
498	
499	def get_optical_pose_base(pinholes: Tensor) -> Tensor:
500	    """Compute extrinsic transformation matrices for pinholes.
501	
502	    Args:
503	        pinholes: tensor of form [fx fy cx cy h w rx ry rz tx ty tz]
504	                           of size (N, 12).
505	
506	    Returns:
507	        tensor of extrinsic transformation matrices of size (N, 4, 4).
508	    """
509	    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
510	        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
511	    raise NotImplementedError
512	
513	def homography_i_H_ref(pinhole_i: Tensor, pinhole_ref: Tensor) -> Tensor:
514	    """Homography from reference to ith pinhole.
515	
516	    .. note::
517	        The pinhole model is represented in a single vector as follows:
518	
519	        .. math::
520	            pinhole = (f_x, f_y, c_x, c_y, height, width,
521	            r_x, r_y, r_z, t_x, t_y, t_z)
522	
523	        where:
524	            :math:`(r_x, r_y, r_z)` is the rotation vector in angle-axis
525	            convention.
526	
527	            :math:`(t_x, t_y, t_z)` is the translation vector.
528	
529	    .. math::
530	
531	        H_{ref}^{i} = K_{i} * T_{ref}^{i} * K_{ref}^{-1}
532	
533	    Args:
534	        pinhole_i: tensor with pinhole model for ith frame.
535	        pinhole_ref: tensor with pinhole model for reference frame.
536	
537	    Returns:
538	        tensors that convert depth points (u, v, d) from pinhole_ref to pinhole_i.
539	
540	    Shape:
541	        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)` and :math:`(N, 12)`
542	        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`
543	
544	    Example:
545	        pinhole_i = torch.rand(1, 12)    # Nx12
546	        pinhole_ref = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
547	        homography_i_H_ref(pinhole_i, pinhole_ref)  # Nx4x4
548	    """
549	    if not (len(pinhole_i.shape) == 2 and pinhole_i.shape[1] == 12):
550	        raise AssertionError(pinhole_i.shape)
551	    if pinhole_i.shape != pinhole_ref.shape:
552	        raise AssertionError(pinhole_ref.shape)
553	    i_pose_base = get_optical_pose_base(pinhole_i)
554	    ref_pose_base = get_optical_pose_base(pinhole_ref)
555	    i_pose_ref = torch.matmul(i_pose_base, inverse_transformation(ref_pose_base))
556	    return torch.matmul(pinhole_matrix(pinhole_i), torch.matmul(i_pose_ref, inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole_ref)))
557	
558	def pixel2cam(depth: Tensor, intrinsics_inv: Tensor, pixel_coords: Tensor) -> Tensor:

561	    Args:

563	        intrinsics_inv: the inverse intrinsics camera matrix. Shape must be Bx4x4.
564	        pixel_coords: the grid with (u, v, 1) pixel coordinates. Shape must be BxHxWx3.

570	        raise ValueError(f'Input depth has to be in the shape of Bx1xHxW. Got {depth.shape}')
571	    if not len(intrinsics_inv.shape) == 3:
572	        raise ValueError(f'Input intrinsics_inv has to be in the shape of Bx4x4. Got {intrinsics_inv.shape}')
573	    if not len(pixel_coords.shape) == 4 and pixel_coords.shape[3] == 3:
574	        raise ValueError(f'Input pixel_coords has to be in the shape of BxHxWx3. Got {intrinsics_inv.shape}')
575	    cam_coords: Tensor = transform_points(intrinsics_inv[:, None], pixel_coords)
576	    return cam_coords * depth.permute(0, 2, 3, 1)

592	    if not len(dst_proj_src.shape) == 3 and dst_proj_src.shape[-2:] == (4, 4):

598	    u_coord: Tensor = x_coord / (z_coord + eps)
599	    v_coord: Tensor = y_coord / (z_coord + eps)

from typing import Iterable, List, Union
import torch
from kornia.core import Device, Tensor, eye, stack, zeros
from kornia.core.check import KORNIA_CHECK_SAME_DEVICE
from kornia.geometry.conversions import convert_points_from_homogeneous, convert_points_to_homogeneous
from kornia.geometry.linalg import inverse_transformation, transform_points
from kornia.utils.helpers import _torch_inverse_cast

class PinholeCamera:
    """Class that represents a Pinhole Camera model.

    Args:
        intrinsics: tensor with shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`
          containing the full 4x4 camera calibration matrix.
        extrinsics: tensor with shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`
          containing the full 4x4 rotation-translation matrix.
        height: tensor with shape :math:`(B)` containing the image height.
        width: tensor with shape :math:`(B)` containing the image width.

    .. note::
        We assume that the class attributes are in batch form in order to take
        advantage of PyTorch parallelism to boost computing performance.
    """

    def __init__(self, intrinsics: Tensor, extrinsics: Tensor, height: Tensor, width: Tensor) -> None:
        self._check_valid([intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width])
        self._check_valid_params(intrinsics, 'intrinsics')
        self._check_valid_params(extrinsics, 'extrinsics')
        self._check_valid_shape(height, 'height')
        self._check_valid_shape(width, 'width')
        self._check_consistent_device([intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width])
        self.height: Tensor = height
        self.width: Tensor = width
        self._intrinsics: Tensor = intrinsics
        self._extrinsics: Tensor = extrinsics

    @staticmethod
    def _check_valid(data_iter: Iterable[Tensor]) -> bool:
        if not all((data.shape[0] for data in data_iter)):
            raise ValueError('Arguments shapes must match')
        return True

    @staticmethod
    def _check_valid_params(data: Tensor, data_name: str) -> bool:
        if len(data.shape) not in (3, 4) and data.shape[-2:] != (4, 4):
            raise ValueError(f'Argument {data_name} shape must be in the following shape Bx4x4 or BxNx4x4. Got {data.shape}')
        return True

    @staticmethod
    def _check_valid_shape(data: Tensor, data_name: str) -> bool:
        if not len(data.shape) == 1:
            raise ValueError(f'Argument {data_name} shape must be in the following shape B. Got {data.shape}')
        return True

    @staticmethod
    def _check_consistent_device(data_iter: List[Tensor]) -> None:
        first = data_iter[0]
        for data in data_iter:
            KORNIA_CHECK_SAME_DEVICE(data, first)

    def device(self) -> torch.device:
        """Returns the device for camera buffers.

        Returns:
            Device type
        """
        return self._intrinsics.device

    @property
    def intrinsics(self) -> Tensor:
        """The full 4x4 intrinsics matrix.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`.
        """
        if not self._check_valid_params(self._intrinsics, 'intrinsics'):
            raise AssertionError
        return self._intrinsics

    @property
    def extrinsics(self) -> Tensor:
        """The full 4x4 extrinsics matrix.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`.
        """
        if not self._check_valid_params(self._extrinsics, 'extrinsics'):
            raise AssertionError
        return self._extrinsics

    @property
    def batch_size(self) -> int:
        """Return the batch size of the storage.

        Returns:
            scalar with the batch size.
        """
        return self.intrinsics.shape[0]

    @property
    def fx(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the focal length in the x-direction.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics[..., 0, 0]

    @property
    def fy(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the focal length in the y-direction.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics[..., 1, 1]

    @property
    def cx(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the x-coordinate of the principal point.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics[..., 0, 2]

    @property
    def cy(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the y-coordinate of the principal point.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics[..., 1, 2]

    @property
    def tx(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the x-coordinate of the translation vector.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., 0, -1]

    @tx.setter
    def tx(self, value: Union[Tensor, float]) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Set the x-coordinate of the translation vector with the given value."""
        self.extrinsics[..., 0, -1] = value
        return self

    @property
    def ty(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the y-coordinate of the translation vector.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., 1, -1]

    @ty.setter
    def ty(self, value: Union[Tensor, float]) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Set the y-coordinate of the translation vector with the given value."""
        self.extrinsics[..., 1, -1] = value
        return self

    @property
    def tz(self) -> Tensor:
        """Returns the z-coordinate of the translation vector.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., 2, -1]

    @tz.setter
    def tz(self, value: Union[Tensor, float]) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Set the y-coordinate of the translation vector with the given value."""
        self.extrinsics[..., 2, -1] = value
        return self

    @property
    def rt_matrix(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the 3x4 rotation-translation matrix.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 4)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., :3, :4]

    @property
    def camera_matrix(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the 3x3 camera matrix containing the intrinsics.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 3)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics[..., :3, :3]

    @property
    def rotation_matrix(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the 3x3 rotation matrix from the extrinsics.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 3)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., :3, :3]

    @property
    def translation_vector(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the translation vector from the extrinsics.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 3, 1)`.
        """
        return self.extrinsics[..., :3, -1:]

    def clone(self) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Return a deep copy of the current object instance."""
        height: Tensor = self.height.clone()
        width: Tensor = self.width.clone()
        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics.clone()
        extrinsics: Tensor = self.extrinsics.clone()
        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width)

    def intrinsics_inverse(self) -> Tensor:
        """Return the inverse of the 4x4 instrisics matrix.

        Returns:
            tensor of shape :math:`(B, 4, 4)`.
        """
        return self.intrinsics.inverse()

    def scale(self, scale_factor: Tensor) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Scale the pinhole model.

        Args:
            scale_factor: a tensor with the scale factor. It has
              to be broadcastable with class members. The expected shape is
              :math:`(B)` or :math:`(1)`.

        Returns:
            the camera model with scaled parameters.
        """
        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics.clone()
        intrinsics[..., 0, 0] *= scale_factor
        intrinsics[..., 1, 1] *= scale_factor
        intrinsics[..., 0, 2] *= scale_factor
        intrinsics[..., 1, 2] *= scale_factor
        height: Tensor = scale_factor * self.height.clone()
        width: Tensor = scale_factor * self.width.clone()
        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, self.extrinsics, height, width)

    def scale_(self, scale_factor: Union[float, Tensor]) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        """Scale the pinhole model in-place.

        Args:
            scale_factor: a tensor with the scale factor. It has
              to be broadcastable with class members. The expected shape is
              :math:`(B)` or :math:`(1)`.

        Returns:
            the camera model with scaled parameters.
        """
        self.intrinsics[..., 0, 0] *= scale_factor
        self.intrinsics[..., 1, 1] *= scale_factor
        self.intrinsics[..., 0, 2] *= scale_factor
        self.intrinsics[..., 1, 2] *= scale_factor
        self.height *= scale_factor
        self.width *= scale_factor
        return self

    def project(self, point_3d: Tensor) -> Tensor:
        '''"Generate the `project` function for the following class to project a 3D point in world coordinates onto the 2D camera plane. The input is `self` and a `point_3d` tensor with shape `(*, 3)`, and the output is a tensor of `(u, v)` camera coordinates with shape `(*, 2)`.

```python
class PinholeCamera:
    def __init__(self, intrinsics: Tensor, extrinsics: Tensor, height: Tensor, width: Tensor) -> None:
        self.height: Tensor = height
        self.width: Tensor = width
        self._intrinsics: Tensor = intrinsics
        self._extrinsics: Tensor = extrinsics
```"'''
        raise NotImplementedError('DL-Bench: implement this function')

    def unproject(self, point_2d: Tensor, depth: Tensor) -> Tensor:
        """Unproject a 2d point in 3d.

        Transform coordinates in the pixel frame to the world frame.

        Args:
            point2d: tensor containing the 2d to be projected to
                world coordinates. The shape of the tensor can be :math:`(*, 2)`.
            depth: tensor containing the depth value of each 2d
                points. The tensor shape must be equal to point2d :math:`(*, 1)`.
            normalize: whether to normalize the pointcloud. This
                must be set to `True` when the depth is represented as the Euclidean
                ray length from the camera position.

        Returns:
            tensor of (x, y, z) world coordinates with shape :math:`(*, 3)`.

        Example:
            >>> _ = torch.manual_seed(0)
            >>> x = torch.rand(1, 2)
            >>> depth = torch.ones(1, 1)
            >>> K = torch.eye(4)[None]
            >>> E = torch.eye(4)[None]
            >>> h = torch.ones(1)
            >>> w = torch.ones(1)
            >>> pinhole = kornia.geometry.camera.PinholeCamera(K, E, h, w)
            >>> pinhole.unproject(x, depth)
            tensor([[0.4963, 0.7682, 1.0000]])
        """
        P = self.intrinsics @ self.extrinsics
        P_inv = _torch_inverse_cast(P)
        return transform_points(P_inv, convert_points_to_homogeneous(point_2d) * depth)

    @classmethod
    def from_parameters(self, fx: Tensor, fy: Tensor, cx: Tensor, cy: Tensor, height: int, width: int, tx: Tensor, ty: Tensor, tz: Tensor, batch_size: int, device: Device, dtype: torch.dtype) -> 'PinholeCamera':
        intrinsics = zeros(batch_size, 4, 4, device=device, dtype=dtype)
        intrinsics[..., 0, 0] += fx
        intrinsics[..., 1, 1] += fy
        intrinsics[..., 0, 2] += cx
        intrinsics[..., 1, 2] += cy
        intrinsics[..., 2, 2] += 1.0
        intrinsics[..., 3, 3] += 1.0
        extrinsics = eye(4, device=device, dtype=dtype).repeat(batch_size, 1, 1)
        extrinsics[..., 0, -1] += tx
        extrinsics[..., 1, -1] += ty
        extrinsics[..., 2, -1] += tz
        height_tmp = zeros(batch_size, device=device, dtype=dtype)
        height_tmp[..., 0] += height
        width_tmp = zeros(batch_size, device=device, dtype=dtype)
        width_tmp[..., 0] += width
        return self(intrinsics, extrinsics, height_tmp, width_tmp)

class PinholeCamerasList(PinholeCamera):
    """Class that represents a list of pinhole cameras.

    The class inherits from :class:`~kornia.PinholeCamera` meaning that
    it will keep the same class properties but with an extra dimension.

    .. note::
        The underlying data tensor will be stacked in the first dimension.
        That's it, given a list of two camera instances, the intrinsics tensor
        will have a shape :math:`(B, N, 4, 4)` where :math:`B` is the batch
        size and :math:`N` is the numbers of cameras (in this case two).

    Args:
        pinholes_list: a python tuple or list containing a set of `PinholeCamera` instances.
    """

    def __init__(self, pinholes_list: Iterable[PinholeCamera]) -> None:
        self._initialize_parameters(pinholes_list)

    def _initialize_parameters(self, pinholes: Iterable[PinholeCamera]) -> 'PinholeCamerasList':
        """Initialise the class attributes given a cameras list."""
        if not isinstance(pinholes, (list, tuple)):
            raise TypeError(f'pinhole must of type list or tuple. Got {type(pinholes)}')
        (height, width) = ([], [])
        (intrinsics, extrinsics) = ([], [])
        for pinhole in pinholes:
            if not isinstance(pinhole, PinholeCamera):
                raise TypeError(f'Argument pinhole must be from type PinholeCamera. Got {type(pinhole)}')
            height.append(pinhole.height)
            width.append(pinhole.width)
            intrinsics.append(pinhole.intrinsics)
            extrinsics.append(pinhole.extrinsics)
        self.height: Tensor = stack(height, dim=1)
        self.width: Tensor = stack(width, dim=1)
        self._intrinsics: Tensor = stack(intrinsics, dim=1)
        self._extrinsics: Tensor = stack(extrinsics, dim=1)
        return self

    @property
    def num_cameras(self) -> int:
        """Return the number of pinholes cameras per batch."""
        num_cameras: int = -1
        if self.intrinsics is not None:
            num_cameras = int(self.intrinsics.shape[1])
        return num_cameras

    def get_pinhole(self, idx: int) -> PinholeCamera:
        """Return a PinholeCamera object with parameters such as Bx4x4."""
        height: Tensor = self.height[..., idx]
        width: Tensor = self.width[..., idx]
        intrinsics: Tensor = self.intrinsics[:, idx]
        extrinsics: Tensor = self.extrinsics[:, idx]
        return PinholeCamera(intrinsics, extrinsics, height, width)

def pinhole_matrix(pinholes: Tensor, eps: float=1e-06) -> Tensor:
    """Function that returns the pinhole matrix from a pinhole model.

    .. note::
        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.camera_matrix`.

    Args:
        pinholes: tensor of pinhole models.

    Returns:
        tensor of pinhole matrices.

    Shape:
        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)`
        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`

    Example:
        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
        >>> pinhole = torch.rand(1, 12)    # Nx12
        >>> pinhole_matrix(pinhole)  # Nx4x4
        tensor([[[4.9626e-01, 1.0000e-06, 8.8477e-02, 1.0000e-06],
                 [1.0000e-06, 7.6822e-01, 1.3203e-01, 1.0000e-06],
                 [1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e+00, 1.0000e-06],
                 [1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e-06, 1.0000e+00]]])
    """
    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
    (fx, fy, cx, cy) = torch.chunk(pinholes[..., :4], 4, dim=1)
    k = eye(4, device=pinholes.device, dtype=pinholes.dtype) + eps
    k = k.view(1, 4, 4).repeat(pinholes.shape[0], 1, 1)
    k[..., 0, 0:1] = fx
    k[..., 0, 2:3] = cx
    k[..., 1, 1:2] = fy
    k[..., 1, 2:3] = cy
    return k

def inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole: Tensor, eps: float=1e-06) -> Tensor:
    """Return the inverted pinhole matrix from a pinhole model.

    .. note::
        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.intrinsics_inverse()`.

    Args:
        pinholes: tensor with pinhole models.

    Returns:
        tensor of inverted pinhole matrices.

    Shape:
        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)`
        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`

    Example:
        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
        >>> pinhole = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
        >>> inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole)  # Nx4x4
        tensor([[[ 2.0151,  0.0000, -0.1783,  0.0000],
                 [ 0.0000,  1.3017, -0.1719,  0.0000],
                 [ 0.0000,  0.0000,  1.0000,  0.0000],
                 [ 0.0000,  0.0000,  0.0000,  1.0000]]])
    """
    if not (len(pinhole.shape) == 2 and pinhole.shape[1] == 12):
        raise AssertionError(pinhole.shape)
    (fx, fy, cx, cy) = torch.chunk(pinhole[..., :4], 4, dim=1)
    k = eye(4, device=pinhole.device, dtype=pinhole.dtype)
    k = k.view(1, 4, 4).repeat(pinhole.shape[0], 1, 1)
    k[..., 0, 0:1] = 1.0 / (fx + eps)
    k[..., 1, 1:2] = 1.0 / (fy + eps)
    k[..., 0, 2:3] = -1.0 * cx / (fx + eps)
    k[..., 1, 2:3] = -1.0 * cy / (fy + eps)
    return k

def scale_pinhole(pinholes: Tensor, scale: Tensor) -> Tensor:
    """Scale the pinhole matrix for each pinhole model.

    .. note::
        This method is going to be deprecated in version 0.2 in favour of
        :attr:`kornia.PinholeCamera.scale()`.

    Args:
        pinholes: tensor with the pinhole model.
        scale: tensor of scales.

    Returns:
        tensor of scaled pinholes.

    Shape:
        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)` and :math:`(N, 1)`
        - Output: :math:`(N, 12)`

    Example:
        >>> rng = torch.manual_seed(0)
        >>> pinhole_i = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
        >>> scales = 2.0 * torch.ones(1)   # N
        >>> scale_pinhole(pinhole_i, scales)  # Nx12
        tensor([[0.9925, 1.5364, 0.1770, 0.2641, 0.6148, 1.2682, 0.4901, 0.8964, 0.4556,
                 0.6323, 0.3489, 0.4017]])
    """
    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
    if len(scale.shape) != 1:
        raise AssertionError(scale.shape)
    pinholes_scaled = pinholes.clone()
    pinholes_scaled[..., :6] = pinholes[..., :6] * scale.unsqueeze(-1)
    return pinholes_scaled

def get_optical_pose_base(pinholes: Tensor) -> Tensor:
    """Compute extrinsic transformation matrices for pinholes.

    Args:
        pinholes: tensor of form [fx fy cx cy h w rx ry rz tx ty tz]
                           of size (N, 12).

    Returns:
        tensor of extrinsic transformation matrices of size (N, 4, 4).
    """
    if not (len(pinholes.shape) == 2 and pinholes.shape[1] == 12):
        raise AssertionError(pinholes.shape)
    raise NotImplementedError

def homography_i_H_ref(pinhole_i: Tensor, pinhole_ref: Tensor) -> Tensor:
    """Homography from reference to ith pinhole.

    .. note::
        The pinhole model is represented in a single vector as follows:

        .. math::
            pinhole = (f_x, f_y, c_x, c_y, height, width,
            r_x, r_y, r_z, t_x, t_y, t_z)

        where:
            :math:`(r_x, r_y, r_z)` is the rotation vector in angle-axis
            convention.

            :math:`(t_x, t_y, t_z)` is the translation vector.

    .. math::

        H_{ref}^{i} = K_{i} * T_{ref}^{i} * K_{ref}^{-1}

    Args:
        pinhole_i: tensor with pinhole model for ith frame.
        pinhole_ref: tensor with pinhole model for reference frame.

    Returns:
        tensors that convert depth points (u, v, d) from pinhole_ref to pinhole_i.

    Shape:
        - Input: :math:`(N, 12)` and :math:`(N, 12)`
        - Output: :math:`(N, 4, 4)`

    Example:
        pinhole_i = torch.rand(1, 12)    # Nx12
        pinhole_ref = torch.rand(1, 12)  # Nx12
        homography_i_H_ref(pinhole_i, pinhole_ref)  # Nx4x4
    """
    if not (len(pinhole_i.shape) == 2 and pinhole_i.shape[1] == 12):
        raise AssertionError(pinhole_i.shape)
    if pinhole_i.shape != pinhole_ref.shape:
        raise AssertionError(pinhole_ref.shape)
    i_pose_base = get_optical_pose_base(pinhole_i)
    ref_pose_base = get_optical_pose_base(pinhole_ref)
    i_pose_ref = torch.matmul(i_pose_base, inverse_transformation(ref_pose_base))
    return torch.matmul(pinhole_matrix(pinhole_i), torch.matmul(i_pose_ref, inverse_pinhole_matrix(pinhole_ref)))

def pixel2cam(depth: Tensor, intrinsics_inv: Tensor, pixel_coords: Tensor) -> Tensor:
    """Transform coordinates in the pixel frame to the camera frame.

    Args:
        depth: the source depth maps. Shape must be Bx1xHxW.
        intrinsics_inv: the inverse intrinsics camera matrix. Shape must be Bx4x4.
        pixel_coords: the grid with (u, v, 1) pixel coordinates. Shape must be BxHxWx3.

    Returns:
        tensor of shape BxHxWx3 with (x, y, z) cam coordinates.
    """
    if not len(depth.shape) == 4 and depth.shape[1] == 1:
        raise ValueError(f'Input depth has to be in the shape of Bx1xHxW. Got {depth.shape}')
    if not len(intrinsics_inv.shape) == 3:
        raise ValueError(f'Input intrinsics_inv has to be in the shape of Bx4x4. Got {intrinsics_inv.shape}')
    if not len(pixel_coords.shape) == 4 and pixel_coords.shape[3] == 3:
        raise ValueError(f'Input pixel_coords has to be in the shape of BxHxWx3. Got {intrinsics_inv.shape}')
    cam_coords: Tensor = transform_points(intrinsics_inv[:, None], pixel_coords)
    return cam_coords * depth.permute(0, 2, 3, 1)

def cam2pixel(cam_coords_src: Tensor, dst_proj_src: Tensor, eps: float=1e-12) -> Tensor:
    """Transform coordinates in the camera frame to the pixel frame.

    Args:
        cam_coords: (x, y, z) coordinates defined in the first camera coordinates system. Shape must be BxHxWx3.
        dst_proj_src: the projection matrix between the
          reference and the non reference camera frame. Shape must be Bx4x4.
        eps: small value to avoid division by zero error.

    Returns:
        tensor of shape BxHxWx2 with (u, v) pixel coordinates.
    """
    if not len(cam_coords_src.shape) == 4 and cam_coords_src.shape[3] == 3:
        raise ValueError(f'Input cam_coords_src has to be in the shape of BxHxWx3. Got {cam_coords_src.shape}')
    if not len(dst_proj_src.shape) == 3 and dst_proj_src.shape[-2:] == (4, 4):
        raise ValueError(f'Input dst_proj_src has to be in the shape of Bx4x4. Got {dst_proj_src.shape}')
    point_coords: Tensor = transform_points(dst_proj_src[:, None], cam_coords_src)
    x_coord: Tensor = point_coords[..., 0]
    y_coord: Tensor = point_coords[..., 1]
    z_coord: Tensor = point_coords[..., 2]
    u_coord: Tensor = x_coord / (z_coord + eps)
    v_coord: Tensor = y_coord / (z_coord + eps)
    pixel_coords_dst: Tensor = stack([u_coord, v_coord], dim=-1)
    return pixel_coords_dst
